summary: Mid-size hierarchical Pachacamac registry (92 cords, 5 STRING / 65 INT / 21 EMPTY) at the Museum fur Volkerkunde, Leipzig (SAM 12405). Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED to depth L3 -- one of the rare deep-tree khipus (only UR280 has L5 depth in validated corpus). 15 clusters across 5 colors (AB 34, KB 14, W 9, PR 9, MB 9). Vocabulary is the canonical CEQUE/CADASTRAL signature: qaqa (rock/mountain), kaqa (wall AY), paqa (dawn/east), chi-DIR-ACC (a chi-compound title), and piqaqa (who-TOP+TOP -- reduplicated interrogative emphatic, 'WHO EXACTLY is responsible?'). Clusters 4 and 9 carry HEAVILY HIERARCHIZED L2 subsidiaries (8-9 sub-cords with L3 grandchildren each), encoding nested huaca/ayllu structures within ceque lines. KFG twin KH0489 with 14 declared Ascher sums but 0/10 verified -- LOW arithmetic integrity typical of registries that map territory rather than balance accounts. PROPOSED CLASSIFICATION: ceque_registry. UR203 was the singleton ceque_registry in the validated corpus; UR253 promotes the genre to N=2, both at Pachacamac, both using the canonical _qa toponymic vocabulary + piqa interrogative. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onsets are E, L2, S, L4 -- not L10/L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  92 cords (L1: 57, L2: substantial subsidiaries, L3: deep grandchildren). Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3).
  15 clusters, sizes [7, 1, 7, 2, 1, 4, 1, 12, 1, 1, 8, 4, 6, 2, 2].
  Composition: INT 65 (71 percent), STRING 5, EMPTY 21 (23 percent), L? 1.
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Museum: Museum fur Volkerkunde, LEIPZIG, Germany. Catalog SAM 12405.
  Colors: AB (anonymous beige) 34, KB (black-blue) 14, W (white) 9, PR (purple) 9, MB (medium brown) 9, plus minor LK / RB / SR / 0D / VG / R appearances.
  KFG twin KH0489.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  4/5 STRING cords decoded (80 percent), 1 chi-compound (cord 53) decoded as 'chi-DIR-ACC' (compound morphology -- chima-ta = 'after-DIR-ACC' or a chi-compound name with -ta accusative).
  Special color 0D appears at cord 55 (piqaqa STRING) -- non-standard color marker, treat as a structural flag for the interrogative.

ASCHER LAYER
  14 pendant-pendant sums declared in KFG, 0/10 verified by sum-constraint. Cascade depth = 3.
  No Pass 3A reclassifications. 1 Pass 3C propagation (one summand value uniquely constrained). No Pass 3D repairs.
  LOW Ascher integrity is consistent with a REGISTRY-class document (ceque/cadastral): such documents map relationships and territories, they do not balance arithmetic accounts. The same LOW Ascher signature appears on UR203 (the validated singleton ceque_registry) and on cadastral khipus generally.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  The architecture is the most distinctive feature: 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED to depth L3, with TWO HEAVILY HIERARCHIZED NODE-CORDS:
    Cluster 4 (cord 18, AB INT 14): carries 8 L2 subsidiaries (EMPTY, GG 29, AB 6, AB 4, KB EMPTY, AB 32, GG 32, MB 1) plus one L3 grandchild (AB 1) on the MB child. This is a multi-attribute node with sub-classification.
    Cluster 9 (cord 36, KB EMPTY): the EMPTY parent carries 9 L2 children (AB 3, GG 9, KB EMPTY, AB EMPTY, AB 13, KB EMPTY, AB 21, KB 17, AB 1, AB 1) and 4 L3 grandchildren (SR EMPTY, VG 1, PR 1, AB 4). The EMPTY parent here is a STRUCTURAL NODE -- not a missing data cord, but a cluster header whose children carry the actual data.
  The 5 STRING cords are placed at conspicuous positions:
    Cord 14 (cluster 2): kaqa = wall (AY), with a heavy knot sequence S10 S10 S10 S10 S10 L4 E (numerical 54 + qa morphology overlay).
    Cord 37 (cluster 10): paqa = dawn/east, with knots S10 L6 E (16 + qa).
    Cord 50 (cluster 12): qaqa = rock/mountain, knots E E (just two figure-eights -- the pure qa-qa form).
    Cord 53 (cluster 12): chi-DIR-ACC compound (knots S10 L2 S10 L3 L5 = 12+13+5 with chi-onset reading on first L2).
    Cord 55 (cluster 13, color 0D): piqaqa = who-TOP+TOP -- reduplicated interrogative (knots S10 L9 E E = 19 + qa + qa).

VOCABULARY
  kaqa (cord 14, AB) -- 'wall (AY)' -- cadastral marker.
  paqa (cord 37, AB) -- 'dawn / east' -- cardinal direction marker.
  qaqa (cord 50, AB) -- 'rock / mountain' -- topographic landmark.
  chi-DIR-ACC (cord 53, AB) -- chi-compound with morphological suffixes (-ma DIR + -ta ACC). Could be a TRUTH-NAME compound or a verbal form (chima-ta = 'toward the after / to the derivative').
  piqaqa (cord 55, color 0D) -- 'who? (INTER)' with reduplicated topic marker (-qa-qa). The reduplication intensifies the interrogative: WHO EXACTLY?
  Total unique vocabulary: 5 words. 80 percent dict hits.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onsets are E (qaqa), L2 (chi compounds), S (paqa starts with S-knot prefix), L4 (kaqa). None match L10 / L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  All 4 ceque-cadastral markers (qaqa, kaqa, paqa, chi-compound) occupy the AB color -- AB is the QUALIFIER channel for this document.
  piqaqa (interrogative) is on color 0D (special) -- CALLED OUT by color choice as a marker question, not just a content word.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (anonymous beige, 34 cords): primary IDENTIFIER / QUALIFIER channel. Carries all 4 cadastral markers + most numerical IDs.
  KB (black-blue, 14 cords): phase separator + structural marker. KB-EMPTY cords delimit clusters; KB-INT cords carry small structural codes.
  W (white, 9 cords): MAGNITUDE / measurement channel. Cluster 10 (cords 38-43) is mostly W EMPTYs -- a deliberate pause-block.
  PR (purple, 9 cords): ACTION / MATERIAL channel per validated table. Carries some hierarchical detail in cluster 7 and cluster 11.
  MB (medium brown, 9 cords): annotation / qualifier channel.
  0D (special, 1 cord): ANOMALY color reserved for the piqaqa interrogative -- the question is structurally distinguished from the answers.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR253 is a CEQUE / HUACA REGISTRY at Pachacamac, listing topographic landmarks (rocks qaqa, walls kaqa, eastern positions paqa) and asking WHO is responsible for each (the piqaqa = 'who exactly?' interrogative). The deep-L3 hierarchy encodes the NESTED CEQUE STRUCTURE: each ceque line passes through multiple huacas, each huaca has multiple attributes / responsible parties.
  This is the SECOND ceque_registry document in the validated corpus, joining UR203 (the singleton). UR203 (167 cords, 10+ colors, waqa x7, ceque-line registry) is the LARGER, more polychromatic, ceque-line-organized version. UR253 (92 cords, 5 colors, deep L3 tree) is the SMALLER, more hierarchical version -- possibly a single ayllu's ceque sub-registry within the broader Pachacamac ceque system that UR203 documents at scale.
  The interrogative piqaqa, with its REDUPLICATED topic marker (-qa-qa = topic-topic), is grammatically marked as an EMPHATIC question -- consistent with an oracle / consultation context where the registry asks 'who is RESPONSIBLE' for each huaca, not just 'who'.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0 (cords 1-7): opening header, mixed colors (AB+KB+GG), small INT values (1-22). Sets the document's parameters.
  Cluster 1 (cord 8): single AB cord val=16, isolated transaction or summary.
  Cluster 2 (cords 9-15) [first STRING]: numerical block on MB/KB/AB with kaqa = wall on cord 14 (heavy knot sequence S10 x5 + L4 E). The wall is the first topographic anchor -- the boundary marker.
  Cluster 3 (cords 16-17): MB block with cord 17 carrying L2 child (W val=2).
  Cluster 4 (cord 18, NODE): the first hierarchical NODE cord -- AB val=14 with 8 L2 subsidiaries spanning multiple colors (GG 29, AB 6, AB 4, AB 32, GG 32, MB 1+L3) -- a multi-attribute landmark with cross-color encoding.
  Cluster 5 (cords 18-21): EMPTY + small INTs.
  Cluster 6 (cord 22): single AB cord val=6 with L2 EMPTY on SR (salmon-red).
  Cluster 7 (cords 23-34): mid-document large cluster with the second-color burst (LK 40, MB 4, AB 21/29, PR 24+L2, R 3, PR 1+L2 GG). The PR (purple = action/material) cords here may mark ritual or material-offering aspects of the registered huacas.
  Cluster 8 (cord 35): single MB cord val=106 with L2 child AB val=49 -- a HIGH-MAGNITUDE summary cord.
  Cluster 9 (cord 36, EMPTY NODE): the second hierarchical NODE -- an EMPTY parent with 9 L2 children and L3 grandchildren. Encodes a complex nested structure where the parent is a STRUCTURAL HEADER (no value), and the children carry the data.
  Cluster 10 (cords 37-43) [paqa]: paqa (dawn/east) on cord 37 with L2 AB val=16 child, then 5 W cords (mostly EMPTY, one INT val=2). The eastern direction is marked with a single value (2) and surrounded by deliberate W EMPTYs -- a contemplative / pause section after the eastern declaration.
  Cluster 11 (cords 44-47): W val=4, PR val=1 with L2 STRING child (un-decoded), PR EMPTY closure.
  Cluster 12 (cords 48-53) [qaqa + chi-compound]: PR val=3 + EMPTY + qaqa (rock) on cord 50 + RB val=4 + KB val=5 + chi-DIR-ACC compound on cord 53. The QAQA is the central topographic anchor -- the rock-landmark that the registry pivots around. The chi-compound that follows attaches a name or title to the rock.
  Cluster 13 (cords 54-55) [piqaqa]: AB INT 11 + STRING piqaqa on color 0D. The DOCUMENT'S CENTRAL QUESTION: 'WHO EXACTLY (is responsible)?' on a special color, marked as the structural inquiry.
  Cluster 14 (cords 56-57): closing pair on LK val=6 + AB val=15. Small closing values.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  The CEQUE SYSTEM of Cuzco (and its Pachacamac counterpart) was the canonical Inca organization of sacred space: imaginary lines radiating from the central Coricancha (or Pachacamac temple), each line passing through a sequence of huacas, each huaca cared for by a specific ayllu. Cobo (1653) recorded 328 huacas on 41 ceques in Cuzco. Pachacamac, as the central coast's principal oracle, would have had its own ceque system with a comparable structure (Eeckhout 1999-2017, Marcos 2002).
  A khipu registry of ceque-line huacas would be a TERRITORIAL DOCUMENT mapping the sacred geography rather than a numerical accounting. Such a document would name the topographic landmarks (qaqa = rocks, kaqa = walls, paqa = eastern alignments), list the responsible ayllus or persons (piqaqa = WHO?), and structure the data hierarchically (suyu -> ceque -> huaca -> attributes), exactly matching the L3-deep tree structure observed in UR253.
  The Leipzig Museum fur Volkerkunde holds a Pachacamac collection from German excavations of the early 20th century (probably Reiss-Stubel or contemporaries). UR253 is one piece in this collection.

KEY PASSAGES
  Cord 14 (kaqa): the boundary wall -- first topographic anchor.
  Cord 18 (NODE): the first deep-tree NODE -- a multi-attribute landmark with 8 sub-attributes.
  Cord 36 (EMPTY NODE): the second NODE -- an EMPTY parent encoding a structural header with 9 children + grandchildren.
  Cord 37 (paqa): the eastern declaration -- direction marker.
  Cord 50 (qaqa): the central rock-landmark -- the document's topographic pivot.
  Cord 53 (chi-DIR-ACC): the chi-compound name attached to the rock.
  Cord 55 (piqaqa, color 0D): THE CENTRAL QUESTION -- 'WHO EXACTLY (is responsible)?' on a special color.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - Vocabulary decodings (kaqa, paqa, qaqa, piqaqa) via standard syllabary v3 -- reproducible.
  - Architecture L3 depth and the two NODE structures (cluster 4 cord 18, cluster 9 cord 36): reproducible from OKR.
  - Comparison with UR203 (the validated singleton ceque_registry): both Pachacamac, both use _qa toponymic vocabulary, both have hierarchical structure (UR203 via 10-color polychromy, UR253 via L3 tree depth). UR253 promotes UR203's singleton status to N=2.
  - Cobo 1653 ceque list (328 huacas / 41 ceques) at Cuzco can be cross-referenced with Pachacamac archaeological survey data (Eeckhout 1999-2017).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR253. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 92 cords, 15 clusters, 5 main colors + special 0D, KFG twin KH0489.
  - Per OKR catalog: SAM 12405, Pachacamac, Museum fur Volkerkunde Leipzig.
  - OKR note: tassle-like beginning of main cord with thread-wrapping in MB threads (~2.5 cm).
narrative:
A wall, a rock, a dawn-line, a name, and a question. That is the vocabulary of the entire khipu, five words across ninety-two cords, and they are placed at the structural pivots of a deep tree.

The first STRING cord, at position fourteen, reads kaqa: the wall. It is buried inside cluster two among nine numerical cords, but the knot sequence around it -- five hundred-knots and a four-turn long-knot followed by a figure-eight -- carries weight: this is not just a wall, this is a wall measured. The ceque registry begins with its first boundary anchor.

Then the document gets HIERARCHICAL. At cord eighteen, a beige cord with value fourteen carries eight subsidiary cords, each on different colors -- gray twenty-nine, beige six, beige four, beige thirty-two, gray thirty-two, brown one with a beige grandchild. Eight attributes attached to one landmark. This is not an accounting. This is a topographic profile.

Clusters five through eight pass through small mixed-color records. Cord twenty-two carries a single salmon-red empty subsidiary -- a marked pause, a colored silence in the middle of the registry. Then at cluster seven, the colors burst: gray, brown, beige, then a black cord with the value forty, then purple cords with the values twenty-four and one and three, each carrying its own subsidiary. Purple is the action color, the material color -- these huacas have offerings attached, ritual material recorded against them.

Cord thirty-five reads one hundred and six on brown, with a beige child carrying forty-nine. The document's high-magnitude summary -- a single number that may stand for a whole sub-region's count or a major collective offering.

Then cord thirty-six: an empty parent on black, but with NINE subsidiary cords beneath it and four grandchildren. The empty cord is not a pause here -- it is a STRUCTURAL HEADER, a deep node in the tree that says 'what follows is one nested unit, decompose it.' Inside this node: small numbers, a beige cord with thirteen, a beige cord with twenty-one, two single-value markers each carrying their own grandchildren on different colors (variegated and purple). The deepest part of the tree.

Cord thirty-seven is paqa: the dawn-line, the east-direction, with a beige sixteen attached as subsidiary. Then five empty white cords -- a long held pause after the eastern declaration. The contemplation cluster.

Cord fifty is qaqa: just two figure-eights, the purest possible form of the rock-word. Two qa-knots, no decoration, no measurement -- the central topographic anchor stated as plainly as it can be stated. After it, a four, a five with a beige one as subsidiary, then chi-DIR-ACC on cord fifty-three -- the name attached to the rock, a chi-compound title with case morphology.

And at cord fifty-five, on a color we do not see anywhere else in the document -- a special 0D color reserved for this single cord -- the question: piqaqa. Who? But not just 'who' -- piqaqa carries a doubled topic marker, the -qa repeated for emphasis. WHO EXACTLY is responsible for this? The document asks the question of itself, marked in a unique color so the reader cannot miss it. This is a registry whose central function is to assign responsibility, and the question is left in the document as the framework that organizes everything else.

The closing two cords, fifty-six and fifty-seven, carry small numbers on black and beige -- six and fifteen, perhaps a final tally or a closing reference number.

What we have, then, is a Pachacamac ceque-registry at a smaller scale than UR203 (which uses ten colors and lists explicit waqa cords). UR253 uses five colors and a deep tree, and it lets the topographic vocabulary -- wall, rock, east, name -- carry the meaning while the architecture carries the relationships. The interrogative is the document's central principle: WHO is responsible for this rock, this wall, this dawn-line? The khipu is the answer to that question, woven into the cotton in cluster after cluster, beige cord after beige cord, with the question itself preserved at cord fifty-five so that anyone reading the document would know what it was for.

prior_work.claim: The ceque system organized Inca sacred space: lines radiating from a central temple (Coricancha at Cuzco, the temple of Pachacamac at the central coast), each line passing through a sequence of huacas, each huaca cared for by a specific ayllu.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13; Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco. Brill.

prior_work.claim: Pachacamac as the central coast's principal oracle had its own ceque-system equivalent, mapping huacas across the temple precinct and surrounding territory.
prior_work.citation: Eeckhout, P. (1999, 2008). Pachacamac durant l'Intermediaire Recent; Marcos, J. (2002). Pachacamac in Inca times.

prior_work.claim: qaqa (rock), kaqa (wall AY), paqa (dawn / east), siqa (climb), taqa (boundary), waqa (huaca), chaka (bridge), piqa (who-TOP) form the canonical CEQUE / CADASTRAL vocabulary in the validated khipu corpus.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System -- DOMAIN_GLOSSARIES table. ALBA Project preprint.

prior_work.claim: Reduplicated topic suffix (-qa-qa) intensifies the interrogative or topical force in Quechua morphology.
prior_work.citation: Cerron-Palomino, R. (2003). Linguistica Quechua. CBC, Cuzco; Gonzalez Holguin (1608).

prior_work.claim: UR203 is the validated singleton ceque_registry in the v3 corpus (167 cords, Pachacamac, waqa x7, 10+ colors, 8 of 10 vocabulary items match Cobo 1653 huaca descriptions).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). UR203 deep read -- ceque_registry classification. ALBA Project session brief 2026-03-26.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR253. PROPOSED CLASSIFICATION: ceque_registry. PROMOTES the genre from singleton (UR203 only) to N=2 in the validated corpus.

evidence.test: 4 of 8 canonical ceque markers present (qaqa, kaqa, paqa, piqa-form) plus L3 deep-tree hierarchy

v3_contribution.claim: UR253 is a SMALLER, MORE HIERARCHICAL ceque variant of UR203. UR203 (167 cords, 10 colors, waqa x7) is the LINE-ORGANIZED version; UR253 (92 cords, 5 colors, L3 tree) is the TREE-ORGANIZED version. Two structural strategies for encoding the same ceque-system content.

The interrogative piqaqa with REDUPLICATED topic suffix (-qa-qa = TOP+TOP) on a UNIQUE 0D color is structurally marked as the document's CENTRAL QUESTION: 'WHO EXACTLY is responsible?' This is the explicit articulation of the ceque-registry function (assigning responsibility for huacas to ayllus).

evidence.test: 0D color appears uniquely on piqaqa cord -- color-as-marker convention

v3_contribution.claim: L3 deep-tree architecture with TWO STRUCTURAL NODE-CORDS (cluster 4 cord 18 = AB INT 14 with 8 L2 children; cluster 9 cord 36 = KB EMPTY with 9 L2 + 4 L3 children). The EMPTY parent at cluster 9 is a STRUCTURAL HEADER, not a missing data cord.

evidence.test: two hierarchical nodes carry depth-3 subsidiary trees in a 92-cord khipu

v3_contribution.claim: v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets are E, L2, S, L4 -- none are L10 or L11). Confirms again the v3.1 non-perturbation property on a ceque registry.

companions.note: The validated singleton ceque_registry (167 cords, Pachacamac, 10+ colors, waqa x7, validated against Cobo 1653 huaca descriptions). UR253 is the SECOND ceque_registry, smaller (92 cords) and tree-organized (L3 depth) rather than line-organized (10 colors). UR253 promotes ceque_registry from singleton to N=2.

Pachacamac cadastral_survey (siqa x5 = pyramids with ramps). UR1095 uses the same _qa toponymic vocabulary for cadastral mapping. UR253 uses similar vocabulary for ceque (sacred geography) mapping -- the two genres share the toponymic root system but differ in sacred vs profane orientation.
Pachacamac juridical_oracle. Shares the piqa interrogative pattern with UR253 (juridical 'who-TOP' for accusation; ceque 'who-TOP+TOP' for responsibility assignment).

Pachacamac ritual_oracle (the Hair Khipu). Both UR253 and UR055 are Pachacamac ritual / sacred-territory documents. UR055 is the oracle CONSULTATION; UR253 is the territorial REGISTRY of huacas being consulted.
Pachacamac pilgrimage_register. The pilgrims documented in AS075 visited the huacas listed in UR253's ceque registry. Functionally complementary documents.

Incahuasi administrative_record with depth-5 tree structure (the deepest tree in the validated corpus). UR253 has depth-3 tree structure -- the second-deepest. Both demonstrate that complex hierarchical encoding was a real Inca scribal technique, not unique to a single khipu.

word_reading.note: 4/5 STRING cords with full dict glosses (kaqa, paqa, qaqa, piqaqa). The chi-DIR-ACC compound on cord 53 has morphological structure decoded but no single dict gloss (it is a compound name or verbal form). 80 percent dict hits.

document_type.note: ceque_registry classification rests on: (1) 4 canonical _qa toponymic markers (qaqa, kaqa, paqa, piqaqa-interrogative), (2) Pachacamac provenance (the central-coast oracle equivalent of Cuzco's Coricancha), (3) L3 deep-tree architecture consistent with nested ceque -> huaca -> attribute structure, (4) STRUCTURAL match with UR203 (the validated singleton ceque_registry). Bumped from medium to medium-high because UR253 EXTENDS the ceque genre rather than proposing a brand-new singleton. Cadastral_survey is the closest alternative; ceque is preferred because of the interrogative piqaqa (asking responsibility) and the 0D special color (marked question).

narrative.note: The narrative coherently reads the document as a Pachacamac ceque/huaca registry asking WHO is responsible for each landmark. Internally consistent: each STRING cord occupies a structurally privileged position; the L3 tree carries the nested huaca data; the interrogative is the organizing principle. Externally anchored by Cobo 1653 ceque framework, Zuidema 1964 ceque organization analysis, and Eeckhout/Marcos on Pachacamac sacred geography.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0489 with 0/10 Ascher checksums verified -- LOW arithmetic integrity, but this is EXPECTED for a registry that maps territory rather than balance accounts (same pattern on UR203). 1 L? cord (NULL turns) is a small data gap. Otherwise clean. Cascade depth 3 on the Ascher graph supports the structural-tree interpretation.

sections.title: Opening Header (mixed colors)
sections.reading: Seven small-int cords across AB / KB / GG colors set the document parameters, then a single AB INT 16 (cord 8) at the boundary of cluster 1.

sections.title: First Topographic Anchor: Wall
sections.reading: Numerical block on MB / KB / AB carrying the first topographic STRING marker: kaqa = wall (AY) on cord 14, with a heavy knot sequence (S10 x5 + L4 + E) attaching numerical weight to the wall designation.

key_cords.note: first topographic anchor: the boundary wall

sections.title: First Hierarchical Node + Mid-Document Sequence
sections.reading: Cord 18 is the FIRST DEEP NODE: AB INT 14 with 8 L2 subsidiaries spanning multiple colors (GG / AB / KB / MB), encoding a multi-attribute landmark profile. Cluster 5 returns to small INTs; cluster 6 adds a single AB cord with an SR-color L2 EMPTY (a marked pause).

sections.title: Mid-Document Polychromatic Cluster
sections.reading: Twelve cords on AB / KB / LK / MB / PR / R colors carrying mid-document data. The PR (purple = action / material) cords on 32 and 34 mark ritual or material aspects of the registered huacas. Cord 35 is a single MB INT 106 with L2 child AB 49 -- the document's HIGH-MAGNITUDE summary.

key_cords.note: the only LK (text color) cord -- a textual or governance reference

sections.title: Second Hierarchical Node (EMPTY parent)
sections.reading: Cord 36 is a KB EMPTY parent with 9 L2 children + 4 L3 grandchildren -- the deepest tree node in the document. The EMPTY parent is a STRUCTURAL HEADER, not a missing data cord. Children carry: AB 3 + L3 SR EMPTY, GG 9, KB EMPTY, AB EMPTY, AB 13, KB EMPTY, AB 21, KB 17, AB 1 + L3 VG 1 + L3 PR 1, AB 1 + L3 AB 4.

key_cords.note: SECOND hierarchical node -- empty parent encoding nested structural unit

sections.title: Eastern Declaration + Contemplation Pause
sections.reading: Cord 37 is paqa = dawn / east, with a beige L2 child val=16. After the eastern declaration, five W (white) cords mostly EMPTY -- a long held pause, the contemplation cluster after the directional anchor.

key_cords.note: the eastern direction marker, with attached value 16
the only INT in the white pause-block

sections.title: Central Rock + Chi-Compound Name
sections.reading: Cluster 11 transitions through W INT 4 + PR INT 1 (with L2 STRING child) + PR EMPTY closure. Cluster 12 carries the document's central rock-anchor: qaqa on cord 50 in its purest form (just two figure-eights = E E), preceded by PR INT 3 and followed by RB INT 4, KB INT 5, and the chi-DIR-ACC compound on cord 53. The chi-compound attaches a name or title to the rock.

key_cords.note: the central rock-landmark in its purest form -- two figure-eights
chi-compound name with morphological case suffixes (-ma DIR + -ta ACC) attached to the rock

sections.title: The Central Question (color 0D)
sections.reading: Cord 54 is AB INT 11 with L2 LK EMPTY. Cord 55 is the document's CENTRAL QUESTION: piqaqa = WHO EXACTLY (is responsible)?, on a UNIQUE color 0D not used anywhere else in the document. The reduplicated topic marker (-qa-qa) intensifies the interrogative.

key_cords.note: THE CENTRAL QUESTION -- WHO EXACTLY (is responsible)? on a unique color marker

sections.title: Closing Pair
sections.reading: Two closing INT cords (LK 6, AB 15) -- a small final tally or closing reference number.
